ESTABLISHMENT OF POWER PLANTS IN KERALA
4500. SHRI M. K. RAGHAVAN:
Will the Minister of POWER be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has received any proposal from the Government of
Kerala for establishing power plants in Kerala, if so, the details thereof;
27.03.2025 550
(b) whether the Government has any plan to establish a nuclear power plant in
Kerala, if so,the details thereof; and
(c) whether the Government has received written consent from the State
Government for establishing a nuclear power plant in Kerala, if so, the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF POWER; AND MINISTER OF
STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF NEW AND RENEWABLE ENERGY (SHRI
SHRIPAD YESSO NAIK):
(a): No such proposal has been received in Ministry of Power. However, as per
Section 7 of Electricity Act, 2003 setting up of a power plant is a de-licensed activity
in the country. Any generating company may establish, operate and maintain a
generating station without requiring a license under Electricity Act, 2003 if it
complies with the technical standards relating to connectivity with the grid. The
availability of adequate generation capacity to meet the electricity demand in a
State lies under the purview of the respective State Government/ State Power
Utility. In order to meet the demand of electricity, State may either set up generating
capacity of its own or may invite bid for setting up of new power plant.
(b) and (c): Presently, there is no proposal for setting up of a nuclear power plant
in the State of Kerala. If the Government of Kerala proposes to setup a nuclear
power plant in the State to meet its emerging power demand, the Government of
India will extend all necessary support.
FROZEN FOOD TECHNOLOGY
4501. DR. T. SUMATHY ALIAS THAMIZHACHI THANGAPANDIAN:
27.03.2025 551
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has encouraged the use of nano-technology and
frozen foods to enhance the shelf life and longevity of food products without
compromising on quality and taste;
(b) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor;
(c) whether the Government has any special projects announced for the
establishment of food processing industries involved in nano-technology and frozen
foods technology, if so, the details thereof; and
(d) the funds allocated for the said scheme during the last ten years for the
same?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a) and (b): In order to boost food processing sector, Ministry of Food Processing
Industries (MoFPI) has been incentivizing setting up/expansion of Food Processing
Industries through its Central Sector schemes namely Pradhan Mantri Kisan
SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY) Scheme, Production Linked Incentive Scheme for
Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI) and Centrally Sponsored – Pradhan Mantri
Formalization of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME) Scheme across the
country. These schemes are not region or state specific but demand driven.
In order to promote use of technology including nano and frozen foods
technology adoption in Food Processing Sector, MoFPI extends Grant-in-Aid to
educational and research institutions including Council of Scientific and Industrial
27.03.2025 552
Research (CSIR) recognized Research and Development (R&D) units in private
sector for related demand driven R&D projects through its R&D scheme under
PMKSY.
Under R&D component of the Scheme, financial assistance as grant-in-aid
is provided to Private organizations/ universities/institutions/R&D laboratories and
CSIR recognized R&D units in private sector to the tune of 50% of equipment cost
in general areas and 70% in difficult areas and to various Universities, IITs, Central/
State Government Institutions, Government funded organizations to promote and
undertake demand driven R&D work in food processing sector for product and
process development, design and development of equipment, improved storage,
shelf-life, packaging etc. R&D projects of Government organizations/ Institutions
are eligible for 100% grant-in-aid for the cost of equipment, consumables and
expenditure related to Research Fellows, etc.
80 research projects have been approved since 2017-18 till 28th February,
2025 with an approved subsidy of Rs. 28.75 crore.
In addition, National Institute of Food Technology, Entrepreneurship and
Management (NIFTEM) Kundli and NIFTEM, Thanjavur, under administrative
control of MoFPI, are also engaged in R&D activities in the sector.
Schemes of MoFPI are, inter alia, intended to develop value-added products,
bringing innovative technologies, enhancing productivity, increasing shelf life and
thus reducing wastage of food.
27.03.2025 553
(c) and (d): The details of R&D projects approved by MoFPI in the field of
Nanotechnology for development of Food Processing Sector and fund allotted
since 2016-17 under the R&D component scheme of PMKSY are at STATEMENT.

DEVELOPMENT OF AIRPORTS IN TAMIL NADU
4502. SHRI D. M. KATHIR ANAND:
27.03.2025 555
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the Government has received any request to expedite the completion
of pending works of all the airports in Tamil Nadu and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the reasons for the inordinate delay in completion of works;
(c) the appropriate steps taken by the Government to expedite the pending works
in Chennai, Coimbatore, Madurai, Tiruchirapalli and Thoothukudi airports; and
(d) the time by which the airports at Vellore and Neyveli would be completed and
made operational?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) to (c): In Tamil Nadu, the Airports Authority of India (AAI) has undertaken various
airport development projects, including the modernization of Chennai Airport,
grading of operational area on the southern side of runway and construction of a
drainage system at Madurai Airport, construction of an ATC tower-cum-technical
block at Tiruchirappalli Airport, and the development of a new terminal building
along with runway extension and allied works at Thoothukudi Airport.
The completion timeline for airport projects is dependent on multiple factors,
including land acquisition, regulatory clearances, removal of obstacles, and
financial closure by the respective airport developers.
The modernization of Chennai Airport has faced delays primarily due to
unforeseen natural calamities, including the COVID-19 pandemic, multiple
cyclones, and unusually high rainfall.
27.03.2025 556
To ensure timely implementation of airport expansion and modernization
projects, regular monitoring is conducted at various levels in the Ministry of Civil
Aviation in coordination with relevant stakeholders. The progress of upgradation
works and the removal of bottlenecks are systematically tracked to mitigate delays.
(d): The development works at Neyveli and Vellore airports have been completed.
The operationalization of these airports depends on several factors, such as
regulatory approvals, licensing, operational readiness, etc.
NHPC POWER STATIONS/PLANTS IN NORTH EASTERN REGION
4503. DR. ANGOMCHA BIMOL AKOIJAM:
Will the Minister of POWER be pleased to state:
(a) the total number of National Hydroelectric Power Corporation (NHPC) power
Stations/plants currently operational in the North Eastern region along with their
location and power generation capacity;
(b) whether there are any plans to decommission any NHPC power
stations/plants in the region and if so, the details thereof including the reasons for
such decommissioning;
(c) the total power output generated by NHPC in the North Eastern Region over
the last five years, State and year-wise;
(d) the number of local jobs generated by NHPC power stations/plants in the
region, both direct and indirect employment; and
(e) the future plans of NHPC in the North Eastern region of the country including
any proposed new projects, expansions or modernization efforts?
27.03.2025 557
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF POWER; AND MINISTER OF
STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF NEW AND RENEWABLE ENERGY (SHRI
SHRIPAD YESSO NAIK):
(a) and (c): NHPC operates following three hydroelectric (HE) power stations in
North Eastern region aggregating to 675 MW capacity and has generated 16935
Million Units (MU) of electricity in past five years:

है।

MULTI MODAL LOGISTICS PARKS IN KARNATAKA
4505. CAPTAIN BRIJESH CHOWTA:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
27.03.2025 561
(a) whether the Government plans to establish a Multi Modal Logistics Park (MMLP)
in Dakshina Kannada under Phase-II of the program, considering its strategic
importance as a key logistics hub on the west coast, if so, the details thereof;
(b) the current status of the development of MMLPs under Phase-I, including the
number of parks completed and those under construction;
(c) the criteria and roadmap for selecting new nodes under Phase-II of the program;
and
(d) the key outcomes of Phase-I including the total land allocated, reduction in
transit time, cost savings, fuel savings, carbon credit generation and the major
commodities being handled?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN JAIRAM
GADKARI):
(a) to (c): Government had approved 15 locations for development of Multi Modal
Logistics Parks (MMLPs) under Phase I of the Bharatmala Pariyojana. So far, 6
MMLPs have been awarded at Jogighopa, Chennai, lndore, Bengaluru, Nagpur and
Jalna. These MMLPs are yet to be operationalized. No new MMLPs are under
consideration besides the 15 MMLPs already approved.
(d): The outcomes of MMLPs would only be known after their operationalization.

रा(cid:186)य/संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)े(cid:253), िजला और (cid:250)ाम-वार (cid:255)गित के साथ-साथ (cid:250)ामीण घर(cid:332) म(cid:164) नल जल आपूित(cid:170) के
(cid:255)ावधान क(cid:236) ि(cid:214)थित उपल(cid:202)ध कराता है।
एसबीएम (जी) क(cid:236) एक(cid:236)कृत (cid:255)बंधन सूचना (cid:255)णाली (आईएमआईएस) म(cid:164) एसबीएम (जी)
काय(cid:170)कलाप(cid:332) जैसे िक (cid:211)यि(cid:265)गत और सामदु ाियक/पा(cid:229)रवा(cid:229)रक शौचालय(cid:332) के िनमा(cid:170)ण, एसएलड(cid:202)(cid:208)यूएम
अवसंरचना, आईईसी, (cid:177)मता िनमा(cid:170)ण तथा िव(cid:176)ीय (cid:255)गित सिहत (cid:255)शासन संबंधी काय(cid:170)कलाप(cid:332) के (cid:255)ित
रा(cid:186)य(cid:332)/संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) क(cid:236) (cid:255)गित कै (cid:200)चर क(cid:236) जाती है।
STATUS OF PMFME AND ODOP SCHEMES
4507. SHRI PRADEEP PUROHIT:
27.03.2025 565
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:
(a) the status of the Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing
Enterprises (PMFME) and One District One Product (ODOP) schemes in the
country, State-wise;
(b) the details of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in the food
processing industry sector in the country, State-wise;
(c) the status of agro-processing clusters in the country, State-wise; and
(d) the details of the areas where such manufacturing industries have been
established in the country, State-wise?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a): Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) is implementing a centrally
sponsored "Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises
(PMFME) Scheme" for providing financial, technical and business support for
setting up / upgradation of micro food processing enterprises in the country. The
scheme aims to enhance the competitiveness of existing individual micro-
enterprises in the unorganized segment of the food processing industry and
promote formalization of the sector. The status of PMFME Scheme as on
28.02.2025 is as under:
(i). 36 States/ UTs have nominated State Nodal Agencies (SNAs) and also
constituted State Level Approval Committees (SLACs) and District Level
Committees (DLCs).
27.03.2025 566
(ii). One District One Products (ODOPs) for 726 Districts have been approved
in 35 States / UTs.
(iii). Seed Capital amounting to Rs.1101.27 crore sanctioned for 3,27,174 Self
Help Group (SHG) Members.
(iv). 75 Incubation Centers have been approved with an outlay of Rs. 205.35
crore.
(v). 15 State proposals for marketing and branding have been approved.
(vi). 2 National Level Technical Institutes (NLTIs) and 43 State Level Technical
Institutes (SLTIs) in 35 States/UTs have been nominated for capacity
building,.
(vii). 760 Product Training Modules developed containing Presentations,
Videos, Detailed Project Reports (DPRs) and Course content/ Handbook for
the benefit of beneficiaries.
(viii). 672 Master Trainers of 36 States/UTs, 1132 District Level Trainers of 27
States/UTs and 95,726 beneficiaries from 32 States/UTs have been trained.
2452 District Resource Persons (DRPs) have been appointed to provide
handholding support to beneficiaries.
(ix). Credit linked subsidy have been sanctioned to 1,27,758 beneficiaries.
The Scheme primarily adopts One District One Product (ODOP) approach to
reap the benefit of scale in terms of procurement of inputs, availing common
services and marketing of products. As on 28th February 2025, ODOP have
been approved for 726 Districts across 35 States/ UTs under the Scheme as
per the details given in the enclosed Statement-I.
27.03.2025 567
(b): Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) is implementing Central Sector
Scheme- “Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry
(PLISFPI)” to support creation of global food manufacturing champions and support
Indian brands of food products in the international markets with an outlay of Rs.
10,900 crores. The State-wise details of companies approved under the scheme
are given in the enclosed Statement-II.
(c): MoFPI is implementing a Scheme for Creation of Infrastructure for Agro
Processing Clusters (APCs) under Pradhan Mantri Kisan Sampada Yojana
(PMKSY) to promote setting up of agro processing clusters in the country. The
State-wise details of approved agro processing clusters under the scheme are
given in the enclosed Statement-III.
(d): The State-wise details of registered food processing industries (as per Annual
Survey of Industries 2021-22) are given in the enclosed Statement-IV. However,
area-wise details of such industries are not maintained by the MoFPI.

EXTENSION OF ATAL BHUJAL YOJANA IN ANDHRA PRADESH
4508. SHRI G. M. HARISH BALAYOGI:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has any plan to extend the Atal Bhujal Yojana (ABY)
in Andhra Pradesh;
27.03.2025 591
(b) if so, the details of the gram panchayats/administrative blocks/talukas and the
districts identified for the implementation of the said scheme along with the
proposed timeline of its implementation in Andhra Pradesh;
(c) the details of the funds allocated for the implementation of the said scheme in
Andhra Pradesh;
(d) whether the Government proposes to restructure the said scheme as a Centrally
Sponsored scheme and if so, the details thereof indicating the proposed cost-
sharing ratio between the Centre and the State;
(e) whether the Government has assessed the groundwater depletion trends in
Andhra Pradesh and if so, the details thereof along with the specific action plan
formulated for Andhra Pradesh to address the same; and
(f) whether the Government has any plan to integrate technological interventions
such as sensor-based monitoring, GIS mapping and AI-based groundwater
management solutions in Andhra Pradesh and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a) to (d): Atal Bhujal Yojana is a pilot project for participatory ground water
management with a fixed duration and outlay. Given the positive impact of Atal
Bhujal Yojana, this Department has planned its second phase, however, no
decision has been taken in this regard.
(e): Central Ground Water Board (CGWB) monitors groundwater levels throughout
the country including the state of Andhra Pradesh, four times in every year. In order
to assess the long term fluctuation in ground water level in Andhra Pradesh, the
27.03.2025 592
water level data collected by CGWB during November 2024 has been compared
with the decadal mean of November (2014-2023). Such analysis of water level data
indicates that about 66.9% of the wells monitored have registered rise in ground
water levels. District-wise Decadal Water Level Fluctuation with Mean (Post-
Monsoon 2014 to 2023) and Post-monsoon 2024 in respect of Andhra Pradesh is
given in the enclosed Statement.
Water being a State subject, the responsibility of addressing the ground
water related issues lies primarily with the concerned State Governments.
However, the Central Government facilitates the efforts of the State Governments
by way of technical and financial assistance through its various schemes and
projects. In this direction, the important steps taken by the Ministry of Jal Shakti and
other central ministries for improvement of ground water resources of the country,
including for the state of Andhra Pradesh, are given below:-
(i) The Government is implementing Jal Shakti Abhiyan (JSA) in the country
since 2019 which is a mission mode and time bound programme for harvesting the
rainfall and taking up water conservation activities. Currently, JSA 2025 has been
launched in the country, with a special focus on Over-exploited, Critical and Semi-
critical districts (OCS districts). JSA is an umbrella campaign under which various
ground water recharge and conservation related works are being taken up in
convergence with various central and state schemes.
(ii) CGWB has taken up National Aquifer Mapping and Management
Programme (NAQUIM) with an aim to delineate aquifer disposition and their
characterization. Entire mappable area of the country of around 25 lakh sq. km,
27.03.2025 593
including 1.41 lakh sq. km area of Andhra Pradesh, has been mapped under the
scheme and management plans have been shared with the respective
State/District administrations for implementation.
(iii) Master Plan for Artificial Recharge to Groundwater- 2020 has been prepared
by the CGWB for the entire country, including Andhra Pradesh and shared with
States/UTs providing a broad outline for construction of around 1.42 crore rain
water harvesting and artificial recharge structures in the country to harness 185
BCM (Billion cubic meter) of water. For Andhra Pradesh, the Masterplan
recommends around 3.16 lakh structures.
(iv) Department of Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare (DA and FW), GoI, is
implementing Per Drop More Crop Scheme in the country, including Andhra
Pradesh, since 2015-16, which focuses on enhancing water use efficiency at farm
level through Micro Irrigation and better on-farm water management practices to
optimize the use of available water resources. As per available information, an area
of 3.1 lakh Ha has been brought under micro irrigation in Andhra Pradesh during
2019 to 2024.
(v) Mission Amrit Sarovar was launched by the Government of India,
which aimed at developing and rejuvenating at least 75 water bodies in each district
of the country, including Andhra Pradesh for the purpose of increasing water
storage and boosting ground water recharge. As an outcome nearly 69,000 Amrit
Sarovars have been constructed/rejuvenated in the country, with 2,154 in Andhra
Pradesh.
27.03.2025 594
(vi) M/o Jal Shakti is promoting conjunctive use of surface water and
groundwater and to reduce over-dependence on groundwater, surface water based
Major and Medium irrigation projects have been taken up in the country under
PMKSY-AIBP scheme in collaboration with States/UTs.
(f): Realizing the significance of having high frequency data on ground water on
real time basis, this Ministry has taken up the process of installing Digital Water
Level Recorders (DWLRs) with telemetry systems throughout the country under its
various schemes and projects like Ground Water Management and Regulation
(GWM andR) Scheme, National Hydrology Project (NHP) etc. The state
governments are also funded for carrying out the said activity under NHP. So far,
around 783 DWLRs have been installed in the state of Andhra Pradesh under the
above said schemes, which transmit water level data directly from the field to a
central server at high frequency, which facilitates near-real-time access to this data.
Additionally, for the purpose of carrying out the complex exercise of groundwater
resource assessments at regular intervals, a web-based application named India
Groundwater Resource Estimation System (INGRES), a GIS based platform, has
been deployed by CGWB for online data entry, computation and generation of
outputs of groundwater resources.
27.03.2025 595

SERVICE LANES IN NHS
4510. SHRI B. MANICKAM TAGORE:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) the steps that are being taken to ensure proper maintenance and regular repair of
service lanes to prevent potholes and other road conditions that cause accidents on
National Highways (NHs);
(b) the progress of developing service lanes in rural and underserved regions along
NHs in Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Telangana;
(c) the strategies that are being employed to address the lack of proper road markings,
signs and pedestrian facilities on many service lanes, which endanger the safety of
local residents and commuters;
(d) the manner in which the Government plan to ensure that the construction of service
lanes does not encroach upon agricultural land or displace local communities in rural
areas; and
(e) the role being played by the Ministry in coordinating with State and local authorities
to ensure that service lanes are efficiently managed and integrated into broader
transportation planning?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN JAIRAM
GADKARI):
(a): The Government has prioritized the maintenance of existing National Highway
(NH) network, including service roads, and inter-alia evolved a mechanism to ensure
27.03.2025 601
Maintenance and Repair (M&R) of all NHs sections through accountable maintenance
agency.
The M&R of stretches of NHs, where development works have commenced or
Operation, Maintenance and Transfer (OMT) Concessions/ Operation and
Maintenance (O&M) Contracts have been awarded, are the responsibility of the
concerned Concessionaires/ Contractors till the end of the Defect Liability Period
(DLP)/ the Concession Period. Similarly, for NHs stretches undertaken under TOT
(Toll Operate and Transfer) and InvIT (Infrastructure Investment Trust), M&R
responsibility lies with concerned Concessionaire till the end of the Concession
Period.
For all remaining sections of NHs stretches, Government has taken a policy
decision to undertake maintenance works through Performance Based Maintenance
Contract (PBMC) or Short Term Maintenance Contract (STMC).
Repair of identified defects/issues in the road condition, including potholes, as
well as other maintenance/repair works are completed by the
Contractor/Concessionaire within the stipulated timeline as per provisions of the
Contract. Compliance is ensured through regular field reports and penalty provisions
incorporated in the Contract documents for action against defaulting
Contractor/Concessionaire.
(b): Service roads for NHs are generally provided at urban/semi-urban locations,
where local traffic is substantial, to segregate the local traffic, including non-motorized
vehicles, from fast moving traffic on the NHs. Service roads are also provided below
the elevated sections and flyovers/grade separators/Vehicular Under Passes
(VUPs) for facilitating turning movements.
27.03.2025 602
Details of length of service roads constructed and under construction in the
States of Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Telangana, including rural and underserved regions
in such States, are as under:-
S. Constructed Length of Length of Service Roads under
State
No. Service Roads (in Km) Construction (in Km)
1 Kerala 612 364
2 Tamil Nadu 1,903 419
3 Telangana 595 118
(c): Government undertakes road markings with thermoplastic paint and retro
reflective signages for better visibility at night, in accordance with Indian Roads
Congress (IRC) guidelines. Pedestrian safety is enhanced through the footpaths, foot
over bridges and crossings in service lane designs.
Contractor/Concessionaire is responsible for maintenance of the NHs sections,
including the service roads, during the DLP/Concession period. Contractors engaged
for maintenance of the NHs sections, particularly for STMC/PBMC works, ensure that
all facilities including the above are properly maintained.
Further, app based monitoring through National Highways Authority of India
(NHAI) One/Tatpar App facilitates highway project management by Field
officers/Engineers/Contractors/Concessionaires directly from onsite, through digital
reporting of daily and monthly defects, submission of geo-tagged and time-stamped
photos for inspections and digital uploading of test results.
(d): Service roads for NHs are constructed within the Right of Way (ROW) of NH.
Land Acquisition for construction of NHs including service roads is carried out in
accordance with the provisions of the NH Act, 1956.
27.03.2025 603
(e): Construction of service roads for NHs is taken up by the Government, in
coordination with State and Local authorities, as part of scope of development projects
on NHs, from time to time based on traffic density, roadside development/settlement
abutting the Project Highway, congestion, accessibility for local traffic etc.
GREENFIELD AND BROWNFIELD NH PROJECTS
4511. SHRI MAGUNTA SREENIVASULU REDDY:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) the list of Greenfield and Brownfield National Highway (NH) Projects that have
been proposed/approved/under construction and the total amount of funds
allocated/released/utilized thereon during the last five years across the country, year
and State-wise especially district-wise in Andhra Pradesh; and
(b) whether the Government has established any timeline within which it aims to
complete all pending Projects pertaining to Greenfield and Brownfield National
Highway projects and if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN JAIRAM
GADKARI):
(a) and (b): The Government in the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways takes
up construction of Greenfield/Brownfield National Highways (NHs) only after due
approval by the competent authority is accorded to the project proposals. The timeline
for construction is specified in the project contracts, which is generally kept as 2 to 3
years from actual commencement of the project, with provision for extension of
timelines as per contract conditions. The State/UT-wise details of such approved NH
projects under construction in the entire country, including in the state of Andhra
27.03.2025 604
Pradesh, but excluding those which are provisionally or physically completed and
those which are under consideration for termination/foreclosure, are given in the
enclosed Statement-I.
Allocation of funds for development and maintenance of NHs are made
State/Agency wise and not project wise. The State/UT – wise and year-wise details
of funds allocated and expenditure incurred towards development and maintenance
of NHs during the last five financial years, are given in the enclosed Statement-II.
27.03.2025 605
STATEMENT-I
The State/UT-wise details of approved NH projects under construction in
the entire country, including in the state of Andhra Pradesh, but excluding
those which are provisionally or physically completed and those which are

ि(cid:300)प(cid:177)ीय हवाई सेवा समझौते का (cid:255)भाव
4512. (cid:174)ीमती िवजयल(cid:224) मी देवी:
(cid:174)ी (cid:255)भभु ाई नागरभाई वसावा:
(cid:179) या नागर िवमानन म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िकः
(क) िव(cid:214)ता(cid:229)रत ि(cid:300)प(cid:177)ीय हवाई सेवा समझौते के िलए िसंगापुर के अनुरोध क(cid:236) समी(cid:177)ा करते समय सरकार
(cid:300)ारा िकन कारक(cid:332) पर िवचार िकया जा रहा है;
(ख) सरकार (cid:300)ारा उन िविश(cid:310) शत(cid:334) का (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है िजनके अंतग(cid:170)त सरकार िसंगापुर क(cid:236) िवमान कं पिनय(cid:332)
के िलए उड़ान(cid:332) क(cid:236) आवृि(cid:176) म(cid:164) विृ (cid:294) को (cid:214)वीकृित (cid:255)दान करगे ी और इसे अनुमोिदत करने से पूव(cid:170) सरकार
को िकन िवशेष अपे(cid:177)ाओ ंको पूरा करना होगा;
(ग) िसंगापुर के साथ िव(cid:214)ता(cid:229)रत हवाई सेवा समझौते का भारतीय एयरलाइन(cid:332) पर (cid:179)या (cid:255)भाव पड़ेगा;
और
(घ) भारत और िसंगापुर के बीच िवशेष (cid:322)प स े पय(cid:170)टन और (cid:211)यापार के संदभ(cid:170) म(cid:164) बढ़े ह(cid:242)ए हवाई संपक(cid:170) से
होने वाले आिथ(cid:170)क लाभ(cid:332) का (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है?
सहका(cid:229)रता म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा नागर िवमानन म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी मुरलीधर
मोहोल):
(क) से (घ): भारत और िकसी िवदेशी रा(cid:213)(cid:367) के बीच ि(cid:300)प(cid:177)ीय हवाई सेवा समझौते का िव(cid:214)तार एक सतत
(cid:255)ि(cid:248)या है और भारतीय िवमानन (cid:177)े(cid:253) को होने वाले लाभ, उस दशे म(cid:164) भारतीय (cid:255)वािसय(cid:332) क(cid:236) उपि(cid:214)थित,
भारतीय वाहक(cid:332) क(cid:236) भिव(cid:213)य क(cid:236) योजनाए,ं पार(cid:214)प(cid:229)रकता के पहलू, लाभ(cid:332) का संतुलन तथा दोन(cid:332) देश(cid:332) के
बीच अ(cid:198)य (cid:255)ासंिगक कारक(cid:332) के आधार पर समय-समय पर िकया जाता है।
भारत और िसंगापुर सिहत िकसी भी िवदशे ी रा(cid:213)(cid:367) के बीच हवाई संपक(cid:170) बढ़ने से पय(cid:170)टन और

(cid:179)या आवासन और शहरी काय(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िकः

आवासन और शहरी काय(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी तोखन साह(cid:243)):
(क) से (घ): 25 जून 2015 को दशे भर के चयिनत 500 शहर(cid:332) (15 िवलय िकए गए शहर(cid:332) सिहत
485 शहर) और क(cid:214)ब(cid:332) म(cid:164) अटल नवीकरण और शहरी प(cid:229)रवत(cid:170)न िमशन (अमतृ ) क(cid:236) श(cid:321)ु आत क(cid:236) गई। यह

(cid:177)े(cid:253) और पाक(cid:170) ; तथा िबना मोटर वाले शहरी प(cid:229)रवहन के (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) बिु नयादी ढांचे के िवकास पर क(cid:164) ि(cid:254)त है।
अमतृ के अंतग(cid:170)त आने वाले शहर(cid:332) क(cid:236) जानकारी संल(cid:181)न िववरण-I म(cid:164) दी गई है।
अमतृ के अंतग(cid:170)त, संपूण(cid:170) िमशन अविध के िलए सभी रा(cid:186)य(cid:332)/संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) (यूटी) म (cid:164)
प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंके िलए 35,990 करोड़ (cid:321)पये क(cid:236) (cid:255)ितब(cid:294) क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय सहायता सिहत 77,640 करोड़ (cid:321)पये
क(cid:236) रा(cid:186)य वािष(cid:170)क काय(cid:170) योजनाए ं (एसएएपी) अनुमोिदत क(cid:236) गई ह(cid:167)। रा(cid:186)य/संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा दी गई

(cid:179)या जल शि(cid:265) म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िक:
(क) (cid:179)या यह सच ह ै िक यमनु ा नदी क(cid:236) सफाई के िलए अनेक योजनाओ ंके काया(cid:170)(cid:198)वयन और िनिधय(cid:332)

जल शि(cid:265) म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी (डॉ. राज भूषण चौधरी):
(क) और (ख): भारत सरकार ने गंगा और उसक(cid:236) सहायक निदय(cid:332) (यमनु ा सिहत) के पुन(cid:321)(cid:294)ार के िलए
माच(cid:170) 2021 तक पांच वष(cid:170) के िलए 2014-15 म (cid:164) नमािम गंगे काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म (एनजीपी) शु(cid:322) िकया था और इस े

आगे बढ़ाकर माच(cid:170) 2026 तक कर िदया गया है। एनजीपी के तहत, यमुना नदी के िलए 6,407.36 करोड़
(cid:321)पये क(cid:236) लागत से 2,243.25 िमिलयन लीटर (cid:255)ितिदन (एमएलडी) सीवरेज उपचार (cid:177)मता के िनमा(cid:170)ण
के िलए 34 प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंको मंजूरी दी गई है। प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंक(cid:236) सूची संल(cid:181)न िववरण म(cid:164) दी गई है।

सिमित (डीपीसीसी) (cid:300)ारा दी गई जानकारी के अनसु ार, जनवरी 2025 म(cid:164) उपचार म(cid:164) अंतर
822.8 एमएलडी है;
ii. कुछ (cid:214)वीकृत औ(cid:299)ोिगक (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) सामा(cid:198)य बिह:(cid:261)ाव उपचार संयं(cid:253)(cid:332) (सीईटीपी) का अभाव;

(ग) और (घ): नदी क(cid:236) सफाई एक सतत (cid:255)ि(cid:248)या है और भारत सरकार नमािम गंगे काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म के अंतग(cid:170)त

(cid:255)दूषण क(cid:236) चुनौितय(cid:332) से िनपटने म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य सरकार के (cid:255)यास(cid:332) म(cid:164) सहायता कर रही है।
रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय राजधानी (cid:177)े(cid:253) िद(cid:208)ली सरकार िविभ(cid:198)न उपाय(cid:332) पर काम कर रही है, िजनम(cid:164) शािमल ह(cid:167):

ग. िविभ(cid:198)न नाली अवरोधक प(cid:229)रयोजनाए।ं
िद(cid:208)ली जल बोड(cid:170) (डीजेबी) के सभी (cid:255)चिलत एसटीपी क(cid:236) िनगरानी डीपीसीसी (cid:300)ारा (cid:255)(cid:194)येक महीने

है। डीपीसीसी िनयिमत आधार पर िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत मानक(cid:332) को पूरा करने के िलए डीजेबी के साथ सपं क(cid:170) म(cid:164) ह।ै

को पूरा न करने क(cid:236) ि(cid:214)थित म(cid:164) दडं का (cid:255)ावधान है और गैर-अनपु ालन के िलए समय-समय पर भगु तान

नह(cid:233) देती ह(cid:167) तो डीजेबी ट(cid:164)ड(cid:229)रंग से (cid:202)लैकिल(cid:214)ट/िवविज(cid:170)त करने का (cid:255)ावधान है। डीजेबी ने िविभ(cid:198)न साइट(cid:332)
पर चूक करने वाली फम(cid:334) पर कार(cid:170)वाई क(cid:236) है।
इसके अलावा, क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय (cid:255)दूषण िनयं(cid:253)ण बोड(cid:170) (सीपीसीबी) िनयिमत (cid:322)प से नदी जल गुणव(cid:176)ा
मापदडं (cid:332) क(cid:236) िनगरानी करता है और सीवेज उपचार संयं(cid:253) (एसटीपी) का िनरी(cid:177)ण भी करता है और
िद(cid:208)ली म(cid:164) (cid:214)थािपत सीवेज उपचार संयं(cid:253) (एसटीपी) क(cid:236) गैर-अनपु ालन ि(cid:214)थित के संबंध म(cid:164) जल ((cid:255)दूषण
क(cid:236) रोकथाम और िनय(cid:253)ं ण) अिधिनयम, 1974 क(cid:236) धारा 18 (1) (ख) के तहत िद(cid:208)ली (cid:255)दूषण िनय(cid:253)ं ण
सिमित को िदनांक 12.11.2024 को िनद(cid:165)श भी जारी िकए ह(cid:167)।
औ(cid:299)ोिगक (cid:255)दूषण क(cid:236) िनगरानी के िलए, यमनु ा नदी के म(cid:180)ु य धारा म(cid:164) गंभीर (cid:255)दूषणकारी उ(cid:299)ोग(cid:332)
(जीपीआई) का वािष(cid:170)क िनरी(cid:177)ण वष(cid:170) 2020 म (cid:164) शु(cid:322) ह(cid:242)आ। वष(cid:170) 2024 के दौरान, िहंडन सिहत यमनु ा पर
2847 जीपीआई को िनरी(cid:177)ण के चौथे दौर म (cid:164) सूचीब(cid:294) िकया गया है। सभी जीपीआई का िनरी(cid:177)ण िकया
गया है। अब तक, 2717 जीपीआई (यमनु ा के म(cid:180)ु य धारा म(cid:164) 2847 जीपीआई म(cid:164) से) पर कार(cid:170)वाई पूरी हो
चुक(cid:236) है, 1826 जीपीआई अनुपालन कर रहे ह(cid:167), 334 अनुपालन कर रहे ह(cid:167), 303 जीपीआई अ(cid:214)थायी
(cid:322)प से बंद ह(cid:167), और 254 जीपीआई (cid:214)थायी (cid:322)प से बंद ह(cid:167)। अनुपालन करने वाले (334 जीपीआई) म(cid:164) से,
28 जीपीआई को बंद करने के िलए नोिटस जारी िकया गया है और 306 जीपीआई को कारण बताओ
नोिटस जारी िकया गया है। यह देखा गया है िक जीपीआई क(cid:236) अनपु ालन ि(cid:214)थित वष(cid:170) 2020 म(cid:164) 64% स े
बढ़कर वष(cid:170) 2023 म(cid:164) 79% हो गई है।

27.03.2025 632
LEASING OF DELHI GOLF CLUB
4515. SHRI MATHESWARAN V. S.:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the total amount received by the Land and Development Office for leasing
the 170 acre land to Delhi Golf Club;
(b) the reasons for not increasing the lease rate by the Government in
accordance with the market rates;
(c) the list of members to whom the membership was granted on ‘out of turn’
basis from 2010 to till date by the Government; and
(d) the methodology adopted by the Government to grant membership on ‘out
of turn’ basis along with the amount of fees charged for the membership?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) to (d): Delhi Golf Club was established as a municipal course in the early
1930s and was earlier known as Lodhi Golf Club. The lease of the land allotted
earlier has been extended from time to time. The last lease renewal for an area of
179 acres to The Delhi Golf Club was done on 19th July, 2012. The amount received
by way of ground rent, etc. from 2012 to date is Rs. 15,54,27,740/-. Land rates
operated by L&DO are linked to market value and are the basis for determining
lease rates for various uses including clubs. The latest revision for Delhi Golf Club
was done with effect from 01.01.2011. The Delhi Golf Club manages its own affairs
as per its Memorandum of Association/ Articles of Association etc. and also grants
memberships under the same.
27.03.2025 633
KRISHI UDAN SCHEME FOR GUJARAT
4516. SHRI DHAVAL LAXMANBHAI PATEL:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the Government has aimed at assisting farmers in transporting
agricultural products under the 'Krishi UDAN' Scheme and if so, the details thereof;
b) the number of airports in Gujarat that have been integrated under the said
scheme;
(c) whether the Government proposes to include Valsad in the 'Krishi UDAN'
scheme and if so, the details thereof; and
(d) the volume of agricultural produce transported from Valsad under the said
scheme?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a): The Krishi Udan Scheme aims to ensure seamless, cost effective, time-bound
air transportation and associated logistics for all Agri-produce originating especially
from Northeast, hilly and tribal regions of the country, so that it improves their value
realisation. Krishi Udan Scheme is a convergence scheme where eight
Ministries/Departments namely Ministry of Civil Aviation, Department of Agriculture
& Farmers' Welfare, Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying, Department
of Fisheries, Ministry of Food Processing Industries, Department of Commerce,
Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Ministry of Development of North-Eastern Region leverage
27.03.2025 634
their existing schemes to strengthen the logistics for transportation of Agri-
produce.;
By enhancing logistics and supply chains, the scheme aims to strengthen
Indian agriculture and boost farmers' income. The Krishi Udan Scheme covers 58
airports in the country, primarily focusing on 25 airports in North Eastern, Hilly and
Tribal region besides 33 airports in other regions/areas.
(b) to (d): At present, in the State of Gujarat, Jamnagar and Rajkot airport are
covered under Krishi Udan. Krishi Udan is an ongoing Scheme and is reviewed
from time to time in consultation with stakeholders.;
MAHATMA GANDHI INSTITUTE OF RURAL INDUSTRIALIZATION
4517. SHRIMATI DAGGUBATI PURANDESWARI:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be pleased to
state:
(a) whether Mahatma Gandhi Institute for Rural Industrialization (MGIRI) is
working to empower rural communities through promotion of traditional crafts and
sustainable practices, if so, the details thereof and the initiatives taken for
enhancing the capacity of rural industries;
(b) the details of various projects undertaken by MGIRI, including product
development, consultancy, incubation and machinery development and the impact
of these projects on micro enterprises, artisans, spinners and weavers;
(c) the status of the project to reposition MGIRI into a Centre of Excellence (CoE)
for Rural Industrialization including the key components;
27.03.2025 635
(d) the proposal for constructing a Hostel-cum-Guest House at MGIRI and the
manner in which it would support the capacity-building programmes at MGIRI; and
(e) the future plans to further strengthen the role of MGIRI in empowering rural
artisans and entrepreneurs across the country?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR
AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a): Yes sir. Mahatma Gandhi Institute for Rural Industrialization (MGIRI) is
working to empower traditional rural artisans through promotion of traditional crafts
and sustainable practices. The details of the initiatives taken for enhancing the
capacity of rural industries are as under:
(i) MGIRI conducts various Skill Development Programs/Entrepreneurship
Development Programs in various MGIRI developed technologies. About 1000 nos.
of SDP/EDP training program have been conducted benefitting 6414 beneficiaries
to enhance the capacity of rural Industries during the last 10 years (i.e. from FY
2014-15 to FY 2023-24).
(ii) MGIRI has conducted about 100 no. of Field Trial/Dissemination of
Innovative Products/Technologies/Processes during the last 10 years (i.e. from FY
2014-15 to FY 2023-24).
(b): MGIRI has undertaken various project for products/processes/machinery
development. Some of the major technologies/products include Khadi finishing
technologies, Solar Charkha, Panchgavya products, Herbal products, Solar Dryers,
Essential Oil Extraction etc. The details are as follows:
27.03.2025 636
1. MGIRI has developed more than 200 Innovative
Product/Machineries/Processes during the last 10 years (i.e. from FY 2014-15 to
FY 2023-24).
2. MGIRI has established a Livelihood Business Incubation Center (LBI) which
has benefitted more than 500 beneficiaries.
3. Consultancy, Quality Guidance and Testings has been provided by various
divisions of MGIRI and executed more than 70 nos. of externally funded projects
for various Government Departments and Organizations during the last 10 years
(i.e. from FY 2014-15 to FY 2023-24).
MGIRI developed innovative product/Machineries/Processes have helped in
establishment of more than 200 enterprises during the last 10 years (i.e. from FY
2014-15 to FY 2023-24).
The impact of these projects on micro enterprises, artisans, spinners, and
weavers are as follows:
1. MGIRI has developed & standardized various products and technologies in
various types of rural industries and thus has created impact for the establishment
and development of micro enterprises.
2. MGIRI is facilitating livelihood incubation, impacting the Livelihood of local
artisans.
3. MGIRI has organised/participated in various Awareness Programmes,
Product Exhibitions Workshops and Conferences creating awareness which has
impacted the artisans lives.
27.03.2025 637
(c): The project to reposition the Mahatma Gandhi Institute for Rural
Industrialization (MGIRI) as a Centre of Excellence (COE) for Rural Industrialization
has been approved by the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
(MSME). Further, process for allocation of funds is in progress.
Key Components of the Project:
a) Conservation of Heritage Structures and Reuse of Heritage Buildings.
b) Development of Research and Development (R&D) related Infrastructure.
(d): The project for constructing a Hostel cum Guest House at MGIRI for Rural
Industrialization has been approved to supports the capacity-building programs at
MGIRI. The facility will enable MGIRI to accommodate a greater number of
trainees, facilitating more extensive and frequent capacity-building programs aimed
at skill enhancement and technology transfer for rural stakeholders. This will also
facilitate visiting experts and officials, strengthening MGIRI’s capacity to organize
seminars, collaborative events, and high-level discussions focused on advancing
rural industrialization.
(e): The future plans to further strengthen the role of MGIRI in empowering rural
artisans and entrepreneurs across the country are as follows:
Repositioning Mahatma Gandhi Institute for Rural Industrialization (MGIRI)

as a Centre of Excellence (COE) for Rural Industrialization. The initiative aims to
enhance MGIRI’s role in promoting sustainable rural industrialization and
empowering rural communities.
27.03.2025 638
Collaborations: Strengthening partnerships with institutions, NGOs, and

private entities to enhance expertise and resource availability, so as to upgrade and
accelerate the process of rural industrialization.
LOAN TO MSME SECTOR
4518. SHRI A. RAJA:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be pleased to
state:
(a) the details of loan amounts sanctioned by banks for small-scale industries in
MSME sector during the last three years;
(b) the efforts made by the Government to mitigate the difficulties faced by these
units including access to affordable credit, technology, access to market etc.; and
(c) whether any instructions have been issued to Public Sector Undertakings
and Government agencies to give preference in public procurement from MSME
Units and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR
AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a): As informed by Reserve Bank of India, the amount disbursed by the
Scheduled Commercial Banks (SCBs) to MSMEs during the last three financial
year is as follows:
27.03.2025 639
Amount (in Rs. Crore)
Credit Disbursement by SCBs
SI. No. Financial Micro Small Medium Total
Year
1. March-2022 4,63,622.67 4,31,696.01 3,71,346.79 12,66,665.47
2. March-2023 6,43,150.40 5,79,554.50 4,74,033.35 16,96,738.26
3. March-2024 8,95,633.99 7,16,599.44 5,92,221.56 22,04,454.98
Source: Reserve Bank of India.
(b): To support and strengthen the MSMEs Sector, the Government implements
various measures, which inter-alia include measures for access to affordable credit,
technology and marketing support. Some of them are as under:
(i) Credit Guarantee Scheme (CGS) for Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs):
CGS has a provision of collateral free loans of up to a limit of Rs. 500 lakh to MSEs
with guarantee coverage up to 90%. The details of the number of guarantees
provided and the amount of guarantees approved for MSEs under CGS are given
below:
Duration FY-2000-01 to FY-2020-2021 to
FY -2019-2020 FY-2024-2025
Number of Guarantees Approved 43,53,591 64,81,482
Amount of Guarantees Approved (In Rs.
2,28,704 6,55,987
crore)
(ii) Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP) provides
Margin Money subsidy upto 35% for setting up of new micro enterprises in the non-
farm sector with project cost of Rs. 50 lakh and Rs. 20 lakh for Manufacturing and
Services enterprises respectively.
27.03.2025 640
(iii) Special Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (SCLCSS) provides 25%
subsidy to SC/ST MSEs on institutional finance for procurement of Plant &
Machinery/Equipment.
(iv) PM Vishwakarma Yojana provides end-to-end support to artisans and
craftspeople of 18 trades who work with their hands and tools which includes
skilling, modern toolkits, subsidized credit support, marketing support, digital
transaction incentives etc.
(v) Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana provides loans up to Rs. 20 lakh to the non-
corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises.
(vi) The network of Technology Centres/Tool Rooms in the country supports
MSMEs in providing access to advance manufacturing technologies, training &
skilling and business consultancy services to make them competitive. ZED 2.0
Scheme has been launched for improved efficacy of certification levels and for
enhancing quality and competitiveness. To digitally empower MSEs, the Trade
Enablement and Marketing Team Scheme has been launched.
(vii) Public Procurement Policy for Micro and Small Enterprises Order, 2012,
provides an assured market share to Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs).
Procurement and Marketing Support Scheme (PMSS) extends benefits to MSEs
for gaining market access through participation in Trade Fairs/ Exhibitions, Vendor
Development Programmes, adoption of Modern Packaging Technique, e-
Commerce platforms, etc. The number of Enterprises benefitted under various
components of PMSS is as follows:
Financial Year No. of Enterprises benefitted Expenditure (Rs. in Crore)
2020-21 3,276 12.48
27.03.2025 641
2021-22 2,332 5.17
2022-23 22,998 27.49
2023-24 35,000 68.69
(c): The Ministry of MSME notified the implementation of Public Procurement
Policy for Micro and Small Enterprises Order, 2012. As per the said Policy, it is
mandatory for all Central Government Ministries/ Departments/ CPSUs to procure
at least 25% of their annual procurement from MSEs, including 4% from MSEs
owned by SC/ST entrepreneurs and 3% from MSEs owned by women
entrepreneurs.
WOMEN OWNED MSMEs
4519. ADV. PRIYA SAROJ:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be pleased to
state:
(a) the number of women-owned MSMEs operating in Uttar Pradesh and
particularly in the Machhlishahr Lok Sabha Constituency;
(b) whether specific initiatives have been implemented to promote and support
women entrepreneurship in the MSME Sector in Uttar Pradesh and the manner in
which these initiatives align with State-level programmes;
(c) whether measures are being taken to enhance access to finance for women
entrepreneurs in Uttar Pradesh considering that studies indicate a significant
percentage of women-owned MSMEs in the country have not availed formal
institutional financing;
27.03.2025 642
(d) the extent to which the Government schemes have been effective in
promoting women-led MSMEs in Uttar Pradesh and the metrics used to evaluate
their success in comparison to other States' performance; and
(e) the challenges unique to Uttar Pradesh identified, that hinder the growth of
women-owned MSMEs?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR
AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a): The number of women-owned Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
(MSMEs) who have registered on Udyam Registration Portal, including Informal
Micro Enterprises registered on Udyam Assist Platform in the State of Uttar
Pradesh are 21,32,684, including Machhlishahr Lok Sabha Constituency.
(b) to (e): The Central Government supplements the efforts of the State/UT
Government in the country, including the state of Uttar Pradesh, to promote and
support women entrepreneurship in MSMEs through its various schemes,
programmes and policy initiatives. Under many of the Schemes, there are
provisions for special incentives to ensure inclusiveness for women. The details of
initiatives for MSMEs in the country, including in the State of Uttar Pradesh, are
such as:
i. Special drives for registration of women-owned MSMEs.
ii. To benefit women entrepreneurs, the Public Procurement Policy for Micro
and Small Enterprises, Order 2012, as amended in 2018, mandates Central
27.03.2025 643
Ministries /Departments /Undertaking to procure at least 3% of their annual
procurement from women-owned Micro and Small Enterprises.
iii. To support women entrepreneurs under Credit Guarantee Scheme for Micro
and Small Enterprises, 10% concession is given in annual guarantee fees; and
guarantee coverage of 90% is given.
Credit Guarantee Scheme for Micro and Small Enterprises –
Number of Guarantees Approved for women-owned MSMEs in Uttar
Pradesh

2024-25 (till
66,129 4,164
28.02.2025)
iv. The Ministry of MSME implements Prime Minister Employment Generation
Programme, which is a major credit-linked subsidy programme aimed at generating
self-employment opportunities through establishment of micro-enterprises in the
non-farm sector by helping traditional artisans and rural/urban unemployed youth.
Under the programme, the subsidy rate for general category beneficiaries is 15%
in urban areas and 25% in rural areas. For special category beneficiaries, including
SC/ST/OBC/Minorities, Women, Ex-servicemen, Physically Handicapped, and
those in NER, Hill, and Border areas, the subsidy is 25% in urban areas and 35%
in rural areas.

(till 24.03.2025)
v. To encourage entrepreneurship among women, Ministry of MSME
implements ‘Skill Upgradation and Mahila Coir Yojana’ under Coir Vikas Yojana,
which is an exclusive training programme aimed at skill development of women
artisans engaged in the Coir sector.
vi. Participation of women entrepreneurs in trade fairs under Procurement and
Marketing Support Scheme is subsidized 100% vis-a-vis 80% for other
entrepreneurs.
vii. The Ministry of MSME launched the ‘Yashasvini Campaign’ on 27.06.2024. The
objective of the Campaign is to empower women entrepreneurs across India
through various schemes on formalization, access to credit capacity building and
mentorship through awareness generation about these schemes.
viii. A new scheme to provide term loans up to Rs.2 crore to Women, Scheduled
Castes and Scheduled Tribes first-time entrepreneurs has been announced in the
Union Budget 2025 for promotion and development of MSME.
The Ministry of MSME coordinates with all stakeholders including MSMEs,
Industry Associations, State Governments, Member Lending Institutions, MSME-
Development and Facilitation Office (MSME-DFOs), etc., to promote the growth of
MSMEs, in the country, including the State of Uttar Pradesh.

PROJECTS BY NHAI IN CHANDIGARH
4520. SHRI MANISH TEWARI:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of all ongoing and proposed projects by the National Highways
Authority of India (NHAI) in Chandigarh along with the budgetary outlay and
expenditure for the same, year-wise;
(b) whether any proposal has been made for the construction of a flyover at the
Tribune Intersection in Chandigarh to facilitate smoother traffic flow on National
Highway-44 connecting Delhi to Chandigarh;
(c) if so, whether the plan for the Tribune Intersection flyover was sanctioned by
both NHAI and the Chandigarh Union Territory (UT) Administration;
(d) if so, the current status of the proposed flyover project including the timeline
of its progress since its inception; and
(e) the reasons for the prolonged delay of over a decade in the construction of
the flyover despite approvals?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN JAIRAM
GADKARI):
(a): Presently, no National Highway works are under implementation in the Union
Territory (UT) of Chandigarh through National Highway Authority of India
(NHAI). However, approx. 15.275 km length of NH-05 which has already been
developed to 6-lane configuration is entrusted to UT PWD.
(b) to (e): The work of construction of flyover on NH-05 near Tribune Chowk was
approved on 11.02.2019 and awarded on 19.11.2019. However, the Contract
27.03.2025 646
agreement could not be signed due to stay granted by the Punjab & Haryana High
Court, Chandigarh in the matter of CWP-PIL No. 60 of 2019 against the
construction of said flyover vide order dated 20.11.2019.
The stay has now been vacated by the Punjab & Haryana High Court.
Considering the substantial gap of more than 5 years from the date of approval of
project, a revised project report and cost estimate as per updated SOR is under
preparation for approval.
MSE-SPICE SCHEME
4521. SHRI HARIBHAI PATEL:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Government has launched the MSE-SPICE Scheme to enable
Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) to adopt Circular Economy solutions, if so,
details thereof;
(b) the details of the objectives, target sectors and strategies adopted under the
scheme;
(c) the total budget allocated for the scheme and the expected benefits for the
MSE sector;
(d) the number of MSEs assisted under the scheme so far and the total
expenditure incurred as on March 2025; and
(e) whether the Government has identified specific initiatives to encourage
recycling and resource reprocessing in key sectors like plastic, rubber and
electronic waste, if so, details thereof?
27.03.2025 647
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR
AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) and (b): The Government of India launched the MSE-Scheme for Promotion
and Investment in Circular Economy (MSE-SPICE) to enable UDYAM registered
Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) to adopt Circular Economy solutions focusing
on sectors like Plastic, Rubber, Electronics waste, Municipal solid waste and liquid
waste, Compressed biogas, End-to-life vehicles, Scrap metal– ferrous and non-
ferrous, Solar panels, Gypsum, toxic, Hazardous industrial waste, used oil waste to
comply with Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and Waste Recycling targets
set for industries and international environmental goals and improve operational
efficiency. The loan size under the MSE-SPICE Scheme varies based on the
project cost. For Brownfield projects in MSEs, the maximum project cost admissible
under the scheme is ₹ 50 Lakhs, with a 25% subsidy on the cost of Plant and
Machinery only. However, projects costing more than ₹ 50 Lakhs are also
admissible under the scheme, but the subsidy would be limited to a maximum of
₹12.5 Lakhs/unit. The objectives of the Scheme are as under:
 To promote Circular Economy (CE) and incentivize MSEs to adopt CE in the
sector with a focus on notified regulations such as Plastic, Rubber and Electronics
Waste Management etc.
 Enable MSE units to comply with Extended Producers Responsibility (EPR)
and Waste Recycling targets set for industries.
27.03.2025 648
(c): MSE-SPICE Scheme having total budget outlay of ₹ 472.50 Crore to enable
more than 3400 Nos. of MSEs to adopt Circular Economy (CE) solutions.
(d): Total 6 Nos. of Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) have been assisted
under the scheme and total expenditure incurred is ₹ 124.54 lakh.
(e): The scheme encourages recirculation of resources by adopting the strategies
of Circular Economy, which interalia includes; reducing, reusing and recycling in
the key sector like Plastic, Rubber and Electronic waste.

अविध के िलए "बाढ़ (cid:255)बंधन और सीमा (cid:177)े(cid:253) काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म " (एफएमबीएपी) के एक घटक के (cid:322)प म(cid:164) जारी रहा
और इसे 2021 से 2026 क(cid:236) अविध के िलए आगे बढ़ाया गया। झारखंड रा(cid:186)य को एफएमपी घटक के

सी.ड(cid:202)(cid:208)यू.सी. बाढ़ (cid:255)बंधन के गैर-संरचना(cid:194)मक उपाय के (cid:322)प म(cid:164) संबंिधत रा(cid:186)य सरकार(cid:332) को

ALLOCATION UNDER SELF RELIANT INDIA FUND
4524. SHRI ARVIND DHARMAPURI:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be pleased to
state:
27.03.2025 652
(a) the total corpus allocated under Self Reliant India Fund (SRI Fund) and the
number of MSMEs that have benefitted from the scheme so far;
(b) the eligibility criteria for MSMEs to avail funding under SRI Fund;
(c) the distribution of funds under SRI, Sector-wise including allocations to
manufacturing, services and technology-based MSMEs;
(d) whether the Government is collaborating with financial institutions or venture
capital firms to enhance the effectiveness of SRI Fund, if so, the details thereof;
and
(e) whether any tax benefits or incentives are provided to investors participating
in SRI Fund to encourage investment in MSMEs, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR
AND EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) to (c): As a part of Aatmanirbhar Bharat package, Rs 50,000 crore equity
infusion for MSMEs through Fund of Funds was announced in 2020. Under this
fund of Rs. 50,000 crore, there is a provision of Rs.10,000 crore corpus from the
Government of India and Rs.40,000 crore through Private Equity/Venture Capital
funds. As per the Self Reliant India (SRI) Fund guidelines, MSMEs, are eligible for
consideration. The equity support is provided to MSMEs which, after assessment,
are found viable, with high growth trajectory and robust funds flows. Since
inception, as on 28.02.2025, 577 MSMEs have benefitted under SRI Fund. The
sector-wise details of MSMEs benefitted in the country under SRI Fund are as given
below:
27.03.2025 653
Amount (in Rs. Crore)

Source: NSIC Venture Capital Fund Limited.
(d): SRI Fund is a Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) registered
Alternative Investment Fund (AIF). SRI Fund commits to other SEBI registered AIF
Categories I & II. SRI Fund has empanelled 60 Daughter Funds, so far.
(e): Investments in SRI Fund, which is designed to support MSMEs, do not offer
any specific tax benefits or deductions under the Income Tax Act. As per Ministry
of MSME’s notification no. S.O. 4926(E), dated 18.10.2022, non-tax benefits are
extended for 3 years in case of an upward change in status of MSMEs.
IMPACT OF OPEN SKY AGREEMENT
4525. SHRI MALVINDER SINGH KANG:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether it is a fact that the 'Open Sky' policy adopted by the Government is
having an adverse effect on the tourism sector in the country;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether the Government proposes to reconsider its policy;
(d) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor; and
(e) the steps proposed to be taken by the Government to minimize its impact on
the tourism sector?
27.03.2025 654
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) Open Sky Agreements enable the enhancement of the air connectivity between
two countries. They help the designated carriers of both the countries to mount
unlimited operations subject to the availability of slots at respective airports. These
agreements provide the opportunity to the Indian carriers as well as carriers of
foreign countries to ramp up operations between India and the respective countries
leading to enhanced air connectivity, which provides a conducive environment for
growth of the tourism sector.
(b) to (e): Does not arise.

27.03.2025 657

VILLAGES UNDER PMAAGY
4529. SHRI BALASHOWRY VALLABHANENI:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether it is true that the Government has identified 36,430 villages in the
country to help tribals under Pradhan Mantri Adi Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAAGY)
after analyzing data of Mission Antyodaya;
(b) if so, the details of villages selected from Andhra Pradesh, district-wise;
(c) whether there is separate fund allocation for PMAAGY or budget under Tribal
Sub-Plan is being used for this purpose; and
27.03.2025 658
(d) the details of the activities undertaken in the said 36,430 villages, with a
particular reference to Andhra Pradesh?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGA DAS UIKEY):
(a), (c) and (d): ‘Pradhan Mantri Adi Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAAGY)’ was
launched by Government for integrated development of eligible villages with 50%
tribal population and 500 STs across States/UTs selected for undertaking
development activities through convergence approach. . The scheme envisions to
mitigate gaps prominently in 8 sectors of development viz. Road connectivity
(Internal and Inter village /block), Telecom connectivity (Mobile /internet), School,
Anganwadi Centers, Health Sub-Centre, Drinking water facility, Drainage and solid
waste management. There is a separate budget allocation for the scheme of
PMAAGY and an amount of Rs.20.38 lakh per village (total Rs 7276 cr for 36,428
villages) was earmarked under the scheme. The State governments were required
to make Village development plan with convergence of Central STC (Schedule tribe
component) and State TSP (Tribal Sub Plan) funds available with sectorial Central
Govt Ministries and State Govt. In the VDP, the aim is to address gaps with regard
to basic amenities identified through Antodaya Mission, in each selected village .
As on date 17,656 VDPs have been approved and total funds released till date
amounts to Rs.2394.40 Cr
(b): Under the scheme of PMAAGY, 517 Villages have been identified from the
State of Andhra Pradesh. The district wise details are given in the enclosed
Statement. However as Andhra Pradesh did not submit the VDPs for the proposed
27.03.2025 659
village Hence funds under the scheme could not be released to State of Andhra
Pradesh.
STATEMENT
District wise Identified villages for the State of Andhra Pradesh Under the
scheme of PMAAGY
S. No. District Name Total Villages
1. Anantapur 4
2. Chittoor 1
3. East Godavari 69
4. Guntur 6
5. Krishna 7
6. Kurnool 2
7. Prakasam 6
8. Srikakulam 66
9. Visakhapatnam 225
10. Vizianagaram 104
11. West Godavari 26
12. Y.S.R. 1
Total Villages 517
ENSURING EQUITABLE TOLL CHARGES ON NATIONAL HIGHWAYS
4530. SHRI HIBI EDEN:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government would reconsider the methodology for toll tax
calculation to ensure fair pricing for road users, if so, the details thereof and if not,
the reasons therefor;
27.03.2025 660
(b) whether the Government would impose stricter regulations on concessionaires
to ensure proper maintenance of highways, if so, the details thereof and if not, the
reasons therefor;
(c) whether concessionaires are fulfilling their obligations regarding road
maintenance, black spot management and passenger amenities, if so, the details
thereof and if not, the reasons therefor;
(d) whether the exponential increase in revenue collection due to higher vehicle
numbers justifies a reduction in toll rates, if so, the details thereof and if not, the
reasons therefor;
(e) whether the Government would review the existing rules that determine toll tax
on National Highways, if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor; and
(f) whether the base rate of toll fees per kilometer under the NH Fee Rules, 2008,
would be lowered considering the increase in vehicular traffic, if so, the details
thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN JAIRAM
GADKARI):
(a): The extant fee rule (National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and
Collection) Rules, 2008) for levy of Fee for services or benefits rendered on
National Highways, was formulated in the year 2008, ensuring transparency and
certainty in the determination of user fee rates, with a uniform methodology for
calculating user fees across the country and the same fee rule continues to be in
effect.
27.03.2025 661
As such, the user fee is collected at a fee plaza for the use of any section of
National Highway as per the provisions of National Highways Fee Rules, 2008
applicable for the respective category of vehicles uniformly across the country.
(b): Maintenance of National Highways (NHs) is regulated/governed as per
provisions of the respective Concession Agreements. In case of any defaults on
part of Concessionaires, appropriate penal actions are taken including the
execution of works at the risk and cost of the Concessionaire.
(c): The development and maintenance of NHs is a continuous process. The
defects in maintenance are surveyed through periodic inspection by the concerned
contractor/ concessionaire/ consultant. The rectification of ageing infrastructure
over a period of time is dealt as per the Operations and Maintenance (O&M)
requirements specified in the contract/concession agreement duly supported by the
technical guidelines. Penal action is also taken in case of non-fulfilment of
conditions stipulated in contract/concession agreement. Accordingly, efforts are
made to ensure that the National Highways are in a traffic-worthy condition.
(d) to (f): The government undertakes several National Highways projects, including
the widening of existing roads, the development of Greenfield NH projects, to
accommodate growing traffic, improve safety, promote economic growth, and
enhance logistics efficiency. However, over time, the construction cost of National
Highways have also increased due to increasing cost of acquisition of land, inflation
and other related expenses.
Various factors such as Lane Configuration i.e. 2 Lane with paved shoulders/
4 or more lane, Length of Section, Length of Bypasses /Structures /Tunnels and
27.03.2025 662
Type of Vehicle (Car, Bus, Multi Axle Trucks, etc.) are considered in determining
the rate of user fee at a fee plaza for use of a particular section of National Highway,
in accordance with provision of NH Fee rules.
Further, the user fee collected at NH user fee plazas is subject to periodic
revision, primarily to account for changes in the Wholesale Price Index (WPI).
भरतपुर को एनसीआर से बाहर िकया जाना
4531. (cid:174)ी भजन लाल जाटवः
(cid:179)या आवासन और शहरी काय(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िकः
(क) (cid:179)या भरतपुर िजले को रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय राजधानी (cid:177)े(cid:253) (एनसीआर) से बाहर करने क(cid:236) लोग(cid:332) क(cid:236) लंबे समय
से चली आ रही मांग सरकार के िवचाराधीन है और यिद हां, तो त(cid:194)संबंधी (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है;
(ख) (cid:179)या सरकार (cid:177)े(cid:253) के िवकास को (cid:197)यान म(cid:164) रखते ह(cid:242)ए भरतपुर के िनवािसय(cid:332) के िलए कोई िवशेष राहत
नीित लाने क(cid:236) योजना बना रही है और यिद हां, तो त(cid:194)संबंधी (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है; और
(ग) (cid:179)या सरकार भरतपुर के िवकास और इसे एनसीआर से बाहर करने क(cid:236) संभावनाओ ंका अ(cid:197)ययन
करने के िलए इस म(cid:293)ु े पर एक उ(cid:184)च (cid:214)तरीय सिमित गिठत करने क(cid:236) योजना बना रही है और यिद हां, तो
त(cid:194)संबंधी (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है?
आवासन और शहरी काय(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी तोखन साह(cid:243)):

DEVELOPMENT OF FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES
4532. SHRI N. K. PREMACHANDRAN:
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government proposes to introduce an effective mechanism for
the development of food processing industries, if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government introduced any mechanism for effective pricing and
marketing strategies, if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether the Government proposes to ensure the availability of raw materials
to be procured for the food processing industries in a reasonable price and if so,
the details thereof;
(d) whether the Government proposes to extend the financial assistance for
modernization of the food processing industry, if so, the details thereof;
(e) the details regarding the closed down food processing industries due to
COVID-19 pandemic, State-wise; and
(f) whether the Government proposes to introduce any special scheme for
comprehensive revival of the said food processing industries and if so, the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
27.03.2025 664
(a): In order to promote and ensure overall development of food processing sector,
Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) has been incentivizing setting
up/expansion of related infrastructure through its Central Sector schemes namely
Pradhan Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY) Scheme, Production Linked
Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI) and Centrally
Sponsored – Pradhan Mantri Formalization of Micro Food Processing Enterprises
(PMFME) Scheme across the country. These schemes are not region or state
specific but demand driven.
Under PMKSY, credit linked financial assistance (capital subsidy) is provided
to entrepreneurs for setting up of food processing industries with total outlay of Rs
5520 Cr for 15th Finance Commission Cycle.
Under PMFME scheme, financial, technical and business support is provided
for setting up / upgradation of micro food processing enterprises. The scheme is
operational for a period of upto 2025-26 with an outlay of Rs.10,000 Crore.
PLISFPI is, inter alia, intended to support creation of global food
manufacturing champions and support Indian brands of food products in
international market. The Scheme is operational for a period from 2021-22 to 2026-
27 with an outlay of Rs. 10,900 crores.
(b) and (c): Grants upto 50% for branding and marketing support to groups of FPOs/
SHGs/ Cooperatives or a Special Purpose Vehicle of Micro Food Processing
Enterprises is available under related component of PMFME Scheme.
In order to promote local industries, One District One Product (ODOP)
approach under PMFME Scheme has been adopted to reap the benefit of scale in
27.03.2025 665
terms of procurement of inputs, availing common services and marketing of
products. It provides the framework for value chain development and alignment of
support infrastructure. The ODOP are identified by States / UTs based on the
agriculture production, raw material availability, perishability of the product etc.
MoFPI has identified 40 clusters for 10 fruits in 15 states, 97 clusters for 11
vegetables in 25 states and 1 cluster in 1 state for marine under scheme for
Operation Greens under Pradhan Mantri Kisan Sampada Yojana for better
availability of raw material to food processing industries. List of production clusters
is given in the enclosed Statement.
Schemes of MoFPI are, inter alia, intended to develop value-added products,
benefitting and increasing the price of their products and income of the farmers in
the country.
(d): MoFPI aims to promote preservation and processing of food products and
modernization/ capacity enhancement of food processing units and Ministry
extends financial support to prospective entrepreneurs for setting up of different
kinds of food processing, as per respective scheme guidelines.
(e): While some food processing industries faced temporary disruptions and
closures due to Covid Pandemic related restrictions in India, the industry as a whole
did not experience a complete shutdown, and many businesses adapted to
continue operations.
In order to support Food Processing Industry during lockdown phases,
MoFPI had constituted a dedicated team of officers and a COVID grievance cell.
The team continuously monitored the food production and supply chain logistics
27.03.2025 666
etc. and facilitated resolution of 649 COVID related Industry grievances and
supported more than 100 companies with restarting their operations. 12 industry
interactions through video conferences, were also organized with industry
associations, leading processors, exporters, FPOs, retailers and other
stakeholders to resolve and provide maximum benefits to this sector.
As per Annual Survey of Industries 2022-23, the number of registered food
processing units, pre and post Covid-19 in the country is as under -:
Year No. of Registered Units
2018-19 40,579
2019-20 41,484
2020-21 41, 938
2021-22 41,913
2022-23 42,801
(f): In order to ensure overall development of Food Processing Industries in the
country including to deal with challenges arising out of COVID-19, MoFPI has
undertaken a number of measures as part of the Aatmanirbhar Bharat Initiative,
which includes (a) New Centrally Sponsored PMFME Scheme; (b) New Central
Sector PLISFPI; (c) Expansion in scope of transportation and storage subsidy
under Operation Greens scheme from Tomato, Onion and Potato to 41 notified
fruits and vegetables.

(क) उ(cid:176)र (cid:255)देश म(cid:164) जल जीवन िमशन (जेजेएम) क(cid:236) मौजूदा ि(cid:214)थित (cid:179)या है;
(ख) गत तीन वष(cid:334) और चालू वष(cid:170) के दौरान जल जीवन िमशन के अंतग(cid:170)त आवंिटत और जारी क(cid:236) गई
धनरािश का िजला-वार (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है;
(ग) (cid:179)या के (cid:198)(cid:254) सरकार को जल जीवन िमशन के अंतग(cid:170)त धनरािश के आवंटन के संबंध म(cid:164) उ(cid:176)र (cid:255)देश
सरकार से कोई (cid:255)(cid:214)ताव (cid:255)ा(cid:302) ह(cid:242)आ है; और
(घ) यिद हां, तो त(cid:194)संबंधी (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है?
जल शि(cid:265) म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा रेल म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी वी. सोम(cid:198)ना):
(क): भारत सरकार देश के सभी (cid:250)ामीण प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) को पया(cid:170)(cid:302) मा(cid:253)ा म (cid:164)िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत गुणव(cid:176)ा वाली और िनयिमत
तथा दीघ(cid:170)कािलक आधार पर सरु ि(cid:177)त और पीने यो(cid:181)य नल जल क(cid:236) आपूित(cid:170) का (cid:255)ावधान करने के िलए
(cid:255)ितब(cid:294) है। इस िदशा म(cid:164), भारत सरकार न े जल जीवन िमशन (जेजेएम) श(cid:322)ु िकया, िजसे अग(cid:214)त 2019
से उ(cid:176)र (cid:255)देश सिहत रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) क(cid:236) भागीदारी से लागू िकया जा रहा है। भारत सरकार तकनीक(cid:236) और िव(cid:176)ीय
सहायता (cid:255)दान करके रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) क(cid:236) सहायता करती है।

क(cid:236) िदशा म(cid:164) मह(cid:194)वपूण(cid:170) (cid:255)गित ह(cid:242)ई है। अग(cid:214)त 2019 म(cid:164) जल जीवन िमशन क(cid:236) शु(cid:321)आत म(cid:164), के वल 3.23

SMART CITIES MISSION IN HARYANA
4534. KUMARI SELJA:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the time of inception of the Smart Cities Mission and the number of cities
covered in the country so far along with the funds allocated, State-wise;
(b) the details of the funds sanctioned, released and utilised in Haryana, city-
wise; and
(c) whether the Government has any plan to bring other cities of Haryana under
the said mission and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a): Smart Cities Mission (SCM) was launched on 25.06.2015. The Central
Government has a total outlay of ₹48,000 crore for the 100 cities covered under
SCM. The State/City wise details of cities selected and the central financial
assistance claimed are given in the enclosed Statement-I.
27.03.2025 676
(b): As on 04.03.2025, two cities viz. Faridabad and Karnal selected under SCM
from the State of Haryana have been able to claim central financial assistance
amounting to ₹980 crore, of which ₹844 crore have been utilized, details of which
are given in the enclosed Statement-II.
(c): No.
